hero.description: A primary school student passionate about programming, focused on creating practical software tools to bring technology closer to life.

minecraft.title: Minecraft Tool Development
minecraft.description: Focused on Minecraft-related tool development, including launcher management, modpack optimization, Bedrock Edition operations, and more to enhance the gaming experience.

tools.title: Practical System Tools
tools.description: Developing various practical system tools such as compiler installers and environment configuration tools to help developers and players simplify operations.

blog.title: Tech Blog Sharing
blog.description: Regularly updating technical articles, sharing C++ programming experience, project development insights, and practical tool usage tips.

website.title: Websites & Documentation
website.description: Creating personal websites and project documentation to share technical achievements and help more people learn and use open-source projects.

education.title: Educational Programming
education.description: Developing educational programming tools and practice programs to help beginners better learn programming and English skills.

learning.title: Continuous Learning
learning.description: Continuously learning new technologies and developing more practical and efficient tools and applications based on user needs.

projects.title: Projects
projects.description: Explore my open-source projects covering tool development, game assistance, educational software, and more

blog.title: My Tech Blog
blog.description: Documenting the programming journey, sharing development insights, and exploring the tech world
blog.author: Author

cppGuide.title: C++ Complete Beginner's Guide: From Hello World to Structures
cppGuide.excerpt: This is a detailed C++ beginner's guide, starting from the basic Hello World program, gradually explaining core concepts such as variables, data types, control flow, functions, pointers, and structures, suitable for beginners to systematically learn C++ programming.

csharpGuide.title: C# From Beginner to Advanced (Zero to Pro)
csharpGuide.excerpt: A comprehensive C# learning guide covering the complete knowledge system from basic syntax to advanced features. Including variables, data types, control flow, methods, arrays, object-oriented programming, generics, delegate events, LINQ queries, and asynchronous programming, with a complete student management system project.

xamlGuide.title: XAML From Beginner to Advanced (Zero to Pro)
xamlGuide.excerpt: Complete WPF interface development guide, from XAML basic syntax to MVVM architecture pattern. Covering layout system, common controls, resource management, data binding, style templates, animation system, and custom controls, with a complete todo application project demonstrating practical techniques.

about.title: About Me
about.description: Learn about my technical background, project experience, and learning journey

intro.title: Introduction
intro.content: I am a primary school student passionate about programming, focused on creating practical software tools. My main tech stack includes C++, C#, Qt, and WinUI 3. I enjoy developing various utility applications, especially Minecraft-related tools. I believe technology should make life simpler and more fun.

tests.title: Psychology Tests
tests.subtitle: Know Yourself
tests.description: Explore your personality type and mental state based on standard scales
tests.disclaimer: The following tests are for reference only and do not constitute any medical or psychological diagnosis. If needed, please seek help from a professional counselor.

mbti.title: MBTI Personality Test
mbti.description: Based on the Myers-Briggs Type Indicator, identify your preferences across four dimensions through 93 questions, recognizing one of 16 personality types.
mbti.subtitle: Myers-Briggs Type Indicator · 93 questions · ~15 minutes

depression.title: Depression Self-Assessment
depression.description: Based on the PHQ-9 Patient Health Questionnaire, an internationally widely used depression screening tool containing 9 core symptom questions.
depression.subtitle: PHQ-9 Patient Health Questionnaire · 9 questions · ~5 minutes
depression.frequencyInstruction: How often have you experienced the following problems in the past two weeks?
depression.resourcesIntro: Regardless of your test results, if you are experiencing psychological distress, you can contact the following professional resources:
depression.noticeText: PHQ-9 is a screening tool, not a diagnostic tool. Test results cannot replace professional assessment by a psychiatrist or counselor. If you are concerned about your mental health, please seek professional help. If you have thoughts of harming yourself, please immediately call a crisis hotline or go to the nearest emergency room.

questions.q1: Little interest or pleasure in doing things
questions.q3: Trouble falling or staying asleep, or sleeping too much
questions.q4: Feeling tired or having little energy
questions.q6: Feeling bad about yourself - or that you're a failure or have let yourself or your family down
questions.q7: Trouble concentrating on things, such as reading the newspaper or watching television
questions.q8: Moving or speaking so slowly that other people could have noticed. Or the opposite - being so fidgety or restless that you have been moving around a lot more than usual
questions.q9: Thoughts that you would be better off dead or of hurting yourself in some way

sunshine.title: Smiling Depression Test
sunshine.description: Identify 'smiling depression' characteristics—outwardly optimistic but inwardly suffering. Assess this hidden state through 15 questions.
sunshine.subtitle: Smiling Depression Assessment · 15 questions · ~8 minutes
sunshine.instruction: Please answer based on your true feelings and select the option that best describes your state.
sunshine.noticeText: This test is designed based on research characteristics of smiling depression and is not a clinical diagnostic tool. A high score does not mean you have depression, but it is worth taking your inner feelings seriously. If you have long-term feelings of inconsistency between your outer and inner self, consider seeking professional psychological counseling.

questions.q1: I show a cheerful, happy side to others even when I don't feel that way inside
questions.q2: Friends or family think I'm positive and optimistic, but I don't feel that way about myself
questions.q3: When people ask how I'm doing, I habitually reply 'I'm fine'
questions.q4: I worry that if I show my true emotions, it will make those around me worry or be disappointed
questions.q5: I feel like I'm 'performing' a better version of myself than my true state
questions.q6: Beneath my calm or even laughing exterior, I feel an indescribable emptiness
questions.q7: I often feel莫名的 sadness or low mood when I'm alone
questions.q8: I sometimes feel mentally exhausted even though I appear fine on the outside
questions.q9: I feel like no one truly understands my inner feelings
questions.q10: Sometimes I suddenly feel a wave of莫名的 sadness after laughing
questions.q11: Even when I'm not feeling well inside, I can still maintain normal work, study, and social functions
questions.q12: My daily life appears normal on the surface, even more 'capable' than many people
questions.q13: It's hard for others to see that I'm actually struggling inside
questions.q14: I sometimes feel that everything I do has lost meaning, even though I continue to do it
questions.q15: I lack hope for the future, but I don't let others notice this

mask.name: External Mask
mask.desc: The degree to which you hide true emotions and maintain a positive image in front of others

pain.name: Inner Pain
pain.desc: The degree of hidden inner pain, emptiness, and low mood

func.name: Function Maintenance
func.desc: The ability to maintain normal daily functions despite inner distress

meaning.name: Meaninglessness
meaning.desc: The degree of losing a sense of meaning in life without revealing it to others

anxiety.title: Anxiety Self-Assessment
anxiety.description: Based on the GAD-7 Generalized Anxiety Scale, assessing the frequency of anxiety symptoms over the past two weeks with 7 core questions.
anxiety.subtitle: GAD-7 Anxiety Scale · 7 questions · ~3 minutes
anxiety.frequencyInstruction: How often have you experienced the following problems in the past two weeks?
anxiety.noticeText: GAD-7 is a screening tool, not a diagnostic tool. Test results cannot replace professional assessment by a psychiatrist or counselor. If you are concerned about your mental health, please seek professional help.

questions.q1: Feeling nervous, anxious, or on edge
questions.q2: Not being able to stop or control worrying
questions.q3: Worrying too much about different things
questions.q5: Being so restless that it's hard to sit still
questions.q7: Feeling afraid as if something awful might happen

bipolar.title: Bipolar Disorder Screening
bipolar.description: Based on the MDQ Mania Scale, preliminary screening for possible bipolar disorder symptoms through 13 questions.
bipolar.subtitle: MDQ Mania Scale · 13 questions · ~5 minutes

phobia.title: Social Phobia Assessment
phobia.description: Based on the SPIN Social Anxiety Scale, assessing anxiety levels in social situations through 22 questions.
phobia.subtitle: SPIN Social Anxiety Scale · 22 questions · ~7 minutes

ptsd.title: PTSD Assessment
ptsd.description: Based on the PCL-5 scale, assessing stress reactions after traumatic events through 20 questions.
ptsd.subtitle: PCL-5 Scale · 20 questions · ~6 minutes

ocd.title: Obsessive-Compulsive Disorder Assessment
ocd.description: Based on the OCI-R scale, assessing the severity of obsessive thoughts and behaviors through 18 questions.
ocd.subtitle: OCI-R Scale · 18 questions · ~5 minutes

eating.title: Eating Disorder Screening
eating.description: Based on the EAT-26 scale, screening for eating disorder risk factors through 26 questions.
eating.subtitle: EAT-26 Scale · 26 questions · ~6 minutes

substance.title: Substance Use Assessment
substance.description: Based on the AUDIT scale, assessing alcohol and other substance use through 20 questions.
substance.subtitle: AUDIT Scale · 20 questions · ~5 minutes

gambling.title: Gambling Behavior Assessment
gambling.description: Based on the PGSI scale, assessing problem gambling risk levels through 9 questions.
gambling.subtitle: PGSI Scale · 9 questions · ~3 minutes

gaming.title: Gaming Disorder Assessment
gaming.description: Based on the IGD-20 scale, assessing the healthiness of gaming use through 20 questions.
gaming.subtitle: IGD-20 Scale · 20 questions · ~6 minutes

adhd.title: ADHD Assessment
adhd.description: Based on the ASRS scale, assessing possible ADHD symptoms through 18 questions.
adhd.subtitle: ASRS Scale · 18 questions · ~5 minutes
adhd.frequencyInstruction: Please assess how often the following situations have occurred in the past six months:
adhd.noticeText: ASRS is a screening tool and cannot replace professional diagnosis. ADHD is a neurodevelopmental disorder that can usually be significantly improved through medication, behavioral therapy, and skills training. If you suspect you may have ADHD, it is recommended to seek a professional neuropsychiatric assessment.

asd.title: Autism Spectrum Assessment
asd.description: Based on the AQ-10 scale, preliminary screening for autism spectrum disorder characteristics through 10 questions.
asd.subtitle: AQ-10 Scale · 10 questions · ~3 minutes
asd.disclaimer: Please note: This test is only for adult preliminary screening and cannot replace professional diagnosis.
asd.instruction: Please assess how well the following statements match your actual situation:
asd.disclaimerText1: AQ-10 is an extremely simplified preliminary screening tool and cannot replace professional autism spectrum disorder diagnosis. Autism spectrum disorder diagnosis requires detailed developmental history interviews, behavioral observations, and standardized assessments by professional doctors. This test result is for reference only and cannot be used as a diagnostic basis.
asd.disclaimerText2: Autism spectrum disorder is a form of neurodiversity, not a disease. Many people on the autism spectrum have unique abilities and strengths. If you suspect you may have autistic traits, it is recommended to seek professional neurodevelopmental assessment.

bpd.title: Borderline Personality Assessment
bpd.description: Based on the MSI-BPD scale, assessing possible borderline personality disorder characteristics through 10 questions.
bpd.subtitle: MSI-BPD Scale · 10 questions · ~3 minutes
bpd.instruction: Please answer based on past experiences.
bpd.questionInstruction: Please assess whether the following statements apply to you:
bpd.noticeText: MSI-BPD is a screening tool and cannot replace professional diagnosis. Borderline personality disorder is a mental disorder that can be effectively improved through specialized treatments such as Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT). If you experience intense mood swings, relationship difficulties, or self-harm urges, seeking professional help is the right choice.

somatic.title: Somatic Symptom Assessment
somatic.description: Based on the PHQ-15 scale, assessing the severity of somatic symptoms through 15 questions.
somatic.subtitle: PHQ-15 Somatic Symptom Scale · 15 questions · ~4 minutes
somatic.instruction: Please answer based on the past four weeks.
somatic.questionInstruction: Over the past four weeks, how much have the following physical symptoms bothered you?
somatic.medicalNotice1: PHQ-15 is a screening tool and cannot replace medical examination. Somatic symptoms may be caused by physical illness, psychological factors, or both. Potential medical conditions must be ruled out before considering psychological factors. If you have persistent physical symptoms, be sure to consult a doctor for a comprehensive medical evaluation first.
somatic.medicalNotice2: Somatic symptom disorder is a real mental disorder. The physical symptoms experienced by patients are real suffering, not "imagined". Symptoms can usually be significantly improved through cognitive-behavioral therapy, mindfulness, and stress management.

result.disclaimer: MBTI is a personality theory tool. Results reflect tendencies, not absolute categorization. Everyone is complex and unique, and should not be limited by type labels.

mbti.title: MBTI Personality Test
mbti.subtitle: Myers-Briggs Type Indicator · 93 questions · About 15 minutes
mbti.description: The MBTI is a widely used personality assessment tool that categorizes individuals into 16 distinct personality types based on four dimensions: Extraversion/Introversion, Sensing/Intuition, Thinking/Feeling, and Judging/Perceiving. This test helps you understand your personality preferences and how you interact with the world.

tests.footerText: All tests on this page are based on publicly available standard scales for personal reference and self-awareness only. Test results cannot replace professional mental health assessments. If you experience persistent psychological distress, please contact a mental health professional promptly.

emergency.title: Emergency Help
emergency.subtitle: Emergency Numbers · Psychological Aid · Self-Help Guide
emergency.description: When you or someone around you encounters an emergency, please call the appropriate number immediately. This page provides commonly used emergency contact numbers and basic self-help methods. Please bookmark and share with those in need.
emergency.phoneSubtitle: The following numbers are available 24/7. Please dial the appropriate number based on your situation.
emergency.guideSubtitle: During the wait for rescue, correct self-help measures may save lives.
emergency.guideItem1Desc: Staying calm is the most important thing in an emergency. Panic can affect your judgment and ability to act, which may lead to more serious consequences.
emergency.guideItem2Desc: Don't hesitate, call the corresponding emergency number immediately. Time is life, the earlier you seek help, the greater the chance of getting rescue.
emergency.guideItem3Desc: When calling emergency services, clearly and accurately describe the location, situation, and your contact information so that rescue personnel can arrive quickly.
emergency.footerNote: Please save emergency phone numbers in your mobile phone and inform your family and friends. Every second counts in an emergency.

police.title: Police
police.label: (Emergency)
police.desc: Call when encountering criminal or public security cases, traffic accidents, fires, and other emergencies.
police.tip1: Stay calm and clearly state the location of the incident
police.tip2: Briefly describe the nature of the incident
police.tip3: Provide as many details as possible

medical.title: Medical Emergency
medical.label: (Ambulance)
medical.desc: Call for sudden illness, accidental injury, or when emergency medical assistance is needed.
medical.tip1: State the patient's age and gender
medical.tip2: Describe the main symptoms or injuries
medical.tip3: Provide accurate address and contact information

fire.title: Fire Department
fire.label: (Fire Rescue)
fire.desc: Call for fires, gas leaks, hazardous chemical accidents, etc.
fire.tip1: State the fire location and size
fire.tip3: Describe the type of burning material

traffic.title: Traffic Accident
traffic.desc: Handle road traffic accidents and related reports
traffic.tip1: Stay calm and move to a safe location
traffic.tip2: Describe the accident location and vehicle information

sms.title: SMS Alarm Number
sms.desc: Use when voice calls are inconvenient (deaf people, dangerous environments, etc.), need to write clearly the reason and specific address
sms.tip1: Write clearly the emergency type and location
sms.tip2: Keep the message concise and clear

water.title: Water Rescue
water.desc: Ship distress, personnel falling into water and other water emergencies, homophonic 'want shore to save me'
water.tip1: State the exact location and water conditions
water.tip2: Describe the number of people in distress
water.tip3: Report if there are children or elderly people

forest.title: Forest Fire Alarm
forest.desc: Special phone for reporting forest and grassland fires
forest.tip1: State the fire location and size
forest.tip3: Report if there are people nearby

redCross.title: Red Cross Emergency Station
redCross.desc: Medical emergency phone provided in some cities for emergency rescue
redCross.tip1: State the patient's condition and location

sos.title: Emergency Call Center
sos.desc: Emergency call function of some mobile phones, can be dialed without SIM card in some cases
sos.tip1: Press the emergency button or dial 112

weather.title: Weather Forecast
weather.desc: Phone for querying the latest weather information
weather.tip2: Specify the time period you want to know

time.title: Time Service
time.tip1: Dial and wait for the automated service

mental.title: Psychological Support Hotline
mental.label: (Mental Health Hotline)
mental.desc: Seek help for emotional distress, psychological crisis, suicidal thoughts and other psychological problems.

women.title: Women and Children Protection
women.label: (Women's Rights Hotline)
women.desc: Seek help for domestic violence, gender discrimination, women and children's rights violations, etc.

cpr.title: Cardiopulmonary Resuscitation (CPR) Basic Steps
cpr.step1Desc: Gently tap shoulders and call out loudly
cpr.step2Desc: Immediately call 120 or ask others to call
cpr.step3Desc: Observe chest and abdomen for起伏 (5-10 seconds)
cpr.step4a: Location: Midpoint of the line connecting the two nipples

bleeding.title: Wound Hemostasis Methods
bleeding.direct1: Use clean gauze or cloth to directly press the wound
bleeding.direct3: Do not frequently check the wound
bleeding.direct4: If the gauze is saturated, add another layer
bleeding.pressure1: Place multiple layers of gauze on the wound
bleeding.pressure3: Moderate tightness, able to stop bleeding without blocking blood circulation
bleeding.pressure4: Observe the color and temperature of the limb end

fireEscape.title: Fire Escape Points
fireEscape.basic3Desc: Smoke rises, stay close to the ground
fireEscape.basic4: Wet Towel Over Nose and Mouth:
fireEscape.situation1Desc: Do not open the door, block the door gap with wet cloth
fireEscape.situation2Desc: Retreat to balcony or window for help
fireEscape.situation4Desc: Roll on the spot, do not run

mentalCrisis.title: Psychological Crisis Coping Methods
mentalCrisis.self: When you feel a psychological crisis:
mentalCrisis.self2Desc: Tell family or friends about your feelings
mentalCrisis.self3Desc: Stay away from dangerous items and places
mentalCrisis.self4Desc: Listen to music, take deep breaths, count
mentalCrisis.self5Desc: Do not make major decisions when emotions are extreme
mentalCrisis.help: Helping others cope with psychological crisis:
mentalCrisis.help1Desc: Do not judge, do not interrupt
mentalCrisis.help2Desc: Clearly show that you care about the person
mentalCrisis.help3Desc: Do not leave the person alone
mentalCrisis.help4Desc: Encourage or accompany to seek professional help

notice.title: Important Reminder
notice.principleDesc: Stay calm → Ensure safety → Seek help → Provide assistance
notice.infoDesc: Accurate location → Clear situation → Contact information → Maintain call
